content:
# Matthew Chapter 8 — The EMET Bible (Literal)
**Translator:** EMET | **Source:** Codex Sinaiticus (א), 4th century CE | **Source Image:** source-texts/greek-NT/MAT_008_page_001.jpg, MAT_008_page_002.jpg

1. And having come down from the mountain, great crowds followed him.

2. And behold, a leper having come was doing-obeisance to him, saying, "Lord, if you will, you are able to cleanse me."

3. And having stretched out the hand, he touched him, saying, "I will; be cleansed." And immediately the leprosy of him was cleansed.

4. And the Yeshua says to him, "See [that] you tell no one; but go, show yourself to the priest and offer the gift which Mouses commanded, for a testimony to them."

5. And having entered into Kapernaoum, a centurion came to him, calling upon him

6. and saying, "Lord, the servant of me has been laid in the house, a paralytic, terribly tormented."

7. And he says to him, "I, having come, will heal him."

8. And answering, the centurion said, "Lord, I am not worthy that under the roof of me you should enter; but only say a word, and the servant of me will be healed.

9. For I also am a man under authority, having under myself soldiers, and I say to this [one], 'Go,' and he goes, and to another, 'Come,' and he comes, and to the slave of me, 'Do this,' and he does [it]."

10. And having heard, the Yeshua marveled and said to those following, "Amen I say to you, with no one in the Israel so great faith I found.

11. And I say to you that many from east and west will come and will recline with Abraam and Isaak and Iakob in the kingdom of the heavens,

12. but the sons of the kingdom will be thrown out into the outer darkness; there will be the weeping and the gnashing of the teeth."

13. And the Yeshua said to the centurion, "Go; as you believed, let it be done to you." And the servant was healed in that hour.

14. And having come, the Yeshua, into the house of Petros, saw the mother-in-law of him having been laid [down] and fevering.

15. And he touched the hand of her, and the fever left her; and she arose and was serving him.

16. And evening having come, they brought to him many being demonized; and he cast out the spirits with a word, and all those having badly he healed,

17. so that might be fulfilled the [word] having been spoken through Esaias the prophet, saying, "He took the weaknesses of us and the diseases he bore."

18. And having seen the Yeshua a crowd around him, he commanded to depart to the other side.

19. And having come to [him], one scribe said to him, "Teacher, I will follow you wherever you go."

20. And the Yeshua says to him, "The foxes have dens, and the birds of the heaven nests, but the Son of the Man does not have where he may lay the head."

21. And another of the disciples said to him, "Lord, permit me first to go and bury the father of me."

22. But the Yeshua says to him, "Follow me, and let the dead bury the dead of them."

23. And having embarked into the boat, the disciples of him followed him.

24. And behold, a great shaking came in the sea, so that the boat was being covered by the waves; but he was sleeping.

25. And having come, they woke him, saying, "Lord, save [us], we are perishing!"

26. And he says to them, "Why are you cowardly, [ones] of little faith?" Then having risen, he rebuked the winds and the sea, and there came a great calm.

27. And the men marveled, saying, "What sort [of man] is this, that even the winds and the sea obey him?"

28. And having come to the other side, into the country of the Gadarenes, two [men] being demonized met him, coming out of the tombs, exceedingly fierce, so that no one was able to pass through that way.

29. And behold, they cried out, saying, "What [is there] to us and to you, Son of the God? Did you come here before [the] time to torment us?"

30. And there was far from them a herd of many swine feeding.

31. And the demons were calling upon him, saying, "If you cast us out, send us into the herd of the swine."

32. And he said to them, "Go." And having come out, they went into the swine; and behold, the whole herd rushed down the steep-bank into the sea and died in the waters.

33. And the [ones] feeding [them] fled, and having gone into the city, reported all things, and the [things] of those being demonized.

34. And behold, the whole city went out to meet the Yeshua; and having seen him, they called upon [him] that he would depart from the borders of them.

---
## EMET's Textual Notes

1. **Γαδαρηνῶν (v.28):** "Gadarenes" — Sinaiticus reads Gadarenes. Other manuscripts read "Gergesenes" or "Gerasenes." Mark and Luke have "Gerasenes." The locations are different cities in the Decapolis region.

2. **Two demoniacs (v.28):** Matthew has two demoniacs where Mark (5:1-20) and Luke (8:26-39) have one. This is a recurring Matthean pattern (cf. two blind men in 20:30 vs. Mark's one). The Sinaiticus preserves Matthew's "two."

3. **σεισμὸς μέγας (v.24):** "A great shaking" — the word σεισμός (whence "seismic") means earthquake/shaking rather than merely "storm." Matthew's word choice evokes cosmic upheaval, not just bad weather.

4. **ὁ υἱὸς τοῦ ἀνθρώπου (v.20):** "The Son of the Man" — Jesus's characteristic self-designation, drawn from Daniel 7:13-14. EMET preserves the article and the literal form.

5. **"Let the dead bury the dead of them" (v.22):** One of Jesus's most startling statements. The first "dead" is metaphorical (spiritually dead); the second is literal. The demand of discipleship overrides even the most sacred family obligation.

— EMET, AI Translator
